changes_summary: Resolved the major plan contradictions by switching baseline capture to dev-mode build artifacts, replacing the conflicting keybind, pinning timeline-row instrumentation to `TrackListRenderer.tsx` (`SortableRow`), clarifying wrapper-component hook placement, defining name-level aggregation semantics, and tightening validation/coverage requirements (5 components × 3 interactions each).

success_criteria.criterion: `useRenderBudget(name, budget): void` is implemented in `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` with React hooks declared unconditionally, dev-gated runtime telemetry logic, 1s idle reset, and over-budget warning behavior.
`RenderTelemetryOverlay` is mounted only in dev, hidden by default, persisted via `localStorage`, uses a non-conflicting keybind, and shows live per-name count/budget status.
All required surfaces are instrumented, including `TrackListRenderer.tsx` (`SortableRow`), with hook placement in executable inner render functions for wrapped components.

Baseline doc is created at `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` with capture date, commit SHA, and measurements covering at least 5 components with at least 3 interactions per measured component.
Budgets are calibrated to baseline behavior (raised to observed max + 1 where needed) so normal baseline interactions do not produce persistent warnings.
`npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` succeed after telemetry changes.
Production runtime bundles (JS/CSS) in `dist/` contain no `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings when grepped excluding sourcemaps.
Implementation stays within approved paths (`src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and explicitly instrumented component files) with no render-refactor/state-management changes.
Manual dev-mode smoke verifies toggle, persistence, live counts, and synthetic over-budget warning before handoff.

assumptions: `npm run build:dev` (or an equivalent `vite build --mode development`) is available for non-HMR telemetry capture.
`TrackListRenderer.tsx` `SortableRow` remains the correct per-row render surface at execution time.
A non-conflicting global shortcut exists; if collisions are found during audit, the first collision-free fallback is documented and used.
Existing debug/perf diagnostics remain unchanged in M0; any duplicate signal is documented rather than refactored.
